value.title: Post these on Gas Pumps
value.description:
Gas prices up after Iran? Eggs through the roof? Tariffs killing your wallet? You know who did that.

These Trump "I Did That" pointing finger stickers are perfect for gas pumps, grocery store price tags, egg cartons, or anywhere prices have gotten out of hand. Gloss vinyl with vibrant fade-resistant printing.

Available in 10, 25, 50, or 100 pack — plenty to share with friends, use at rallies, or keep the joke going all week long.

PRODUCT FEATURES
- Size: 2" W x 1.75" H
- Premium gloss waterproof and weatherproof vinyl
- 6-year outdoor durability
- Permanent adhesive — sticks to metal, glass, and plastic
- Vibrant, fade-resistant printing
- Simple peel and stick application
- Proudly custom made in the USA

Perfect as a gag gift, protest sticker, or bulk sticker pack for events, rallies, and gatherings. Great for anyone fed up with high grocery prices, gas prices, egg prices, and Trump tariffs.

Trump I Did That Stickers, Gas Pump Sticker, Price Sticker, Trump Tariffs, Political Humor, Waterproof Vinyl Decal

value.title: Anti-Trump Epstein Button
value.description:
This Still Waiting for the Epstein Files pin pairs dark humor with a clear message: accountability still matters. Featuring a bold monochrome skull illustration holding a steaming cup beneath arched lettering, this pin quietly says what plenty of people are thinking — and still waiting to hear answers about.

Whether you’re heading to a rally, organizing with friends, or just adding a little truth-telling energy to your jacket, tote bag or lanyard, this Democratic resistance button keeps the conversation going.

It’s especially at home with folks who follow the details, side-eye the headlines and haven’t forgotten the unanswered questions surrounding the Bondi coverup conversation.

Small button. Big memory.

Why you’ll love it

• Durable metal construction with glossy mylar finish
• Scratch-resistant, vibrant print that holds up through daily wear
• Strong safety-pin backing for secure attachment
• Lightweight and easy to add to jackets, backpacks, hats or totes
• Available in multiple sizes for subtle or statement-making visibility
• Assembled in the USA from globally sourced materials

Perfect for

• Democratic resistance supporters
• Protest events and organizing meetups
• Political humor collectors
• Accountability-minded voters
• Your favorite outspoken Democratic mom
• Anyone still waiting for the Epstein files

Wear it to your closest No Kings rally. Wear it to the school pickup line. Wear it wherever people still believe transparency isn’t optional.

Thank you for supporting our small business and independent political art.

value.title: Order a Red Card
value.description: All people in the United States, regardless of immigration status, have certain rights and protections under the U.S. Constitution. The Know Your Rights Red Cards were created to help people assert their rights and defend themselves against constitutional violations. Knowing and asserting rights can make a huge difference in many situations, such as when ICE agents go to a home. Red cards provide critical information on how to assert these rights, along with an explanation to ICE agents that the individual is indeed asserting their rights.  (Available in 56 languages)

value.title: File a public comment on a Trump-administration federal rule today
value.description:
1. Open regulations.gov; sort by Closing Soon.
2. Pick one rule directly affecting an issue you care about.
3. Write 200-300 original words on local impact — form-letter comments are deweighted.
4. Click Submit. Screenshot the receipt.

value.description: Trump's $45B ICE expansion requires private landlords to take the contracts — Sabey Corp signed a new Tukwila WA lease. Indivisible Southend rallies outside the property every other Wednesday, 4pm PDT. Show up, make the lease politically toxic.

value.title: Plug into 50501's Virtual Actions Hub
value.description: 50501's central page for digital direct actions you can do from home — signal-boost campaigns, virtual call days, and online resistance against the Trump administration. One-stop entry point that updates weekly.

value.description: Mijente's flagship organizing campaign pressuring tech companies (Palantir, Amazon, Microsoft) to stop building surveillance and ICE tooling that powers Trump's deportation machine. Sign on as a tech worker, student, or ally.

value.title: Take Action with Spellbound Stitchery's Donate-to-Enter Craftivism Raffles

value.title: Urge Democrats to Oppose New GOP Effort to Shovel Billions to ICE

1. Follow @icewatchapp.bsky.social on Bluesky.
2. Download the ICE Watch app from their pinned link.
3. Allow location and notification permissions.
4. Set your county as the alert region.
5. Share verified alerts with your local rapid-response network.

value.title: Call your bank's branch and read the Stop the Money Pipeline script
value.description:
1. Follow @stopmoneypipeline.bsky.social.
2. Open their pinned script for this month's target bank.
3. Call your local branch (not corporate).
4. Read the script in 90 seconds; thank the branch manager.
5. Post on Bluesky that you called and tag the bank.

value.title: Show up to the next Hands Off NYC monthly rally
value.description:
1. Follow @handsoffnyc.bsky.social.
2. RSVP the next monthly rally.
3. Bring 2 friends.
4. Sign in at the marshals table.
5. Stay through the post-rally next-steps meeting.

value.title: Join Tesla Takedown Baltimore at the Owings Mills showroom
value.description:
1. Follow @teslatakedownbmore.bsky.social.
2. Pick the next listed Saturday.
3. Drive to Owings Mills showroom.
4. Bring a sign; sign in with marshals.
5. Post a Bluesky check-in tagged at the chapter.

value.title: RSVP to the next Hands Off weekly mass call via @handsoffnow
value.description:
1. Follow @handsoffnow.bsky.social.
2. Click the latest pinned mass-call RSVP link.
3. Block 1 hour on your calendar.
4. Show up; take notes.
5. Repost the call's three action asks within an hour after it ends.

value.title: Add the Boldly Go Seattle event feed to your calendar
value.description:
1. Follow @boldlygoseattle.bsky.social.
2. Each week, save the events from their pinned posts to your calendar.
3. Show up to one event per month minimum.
4. Bring a friend.
5. Post a recap on Bluesky after the event.

value.title: Show up to the next Hands Off Chicago Loop rally
value.description:
1. Follow @handsoffchicago.bsky.social.
2. Find next pinned date in feed.
3. Take CTA red or blue to Federal Plaza.
4. Bring a sign.
5. Stay through the marshal-led breakouts.

value.title: Show up to MN 50501's St. Paul capitol day
value.location: In person — MN State Capitol, St. Paul
value.description:
1. Follow @mn50501.bsky.social.
2. RSVP the next pinned capitol day.
3. Carpool from Minneapolis-St. Paul, Duluth, or Rochester.
4. Bring a sign.
5. Stay through the legislator-meet session.

value.title: RSVP to a Black 7 court date and show up to court watch
value.location: In person — federal courthouse hosting the trial
value.description:
1. Follow @handsoffblack7.bsky.social.
2. Find the next hearing date on a pinned post.
3. RSVP via the linked form.
4. Arrive 30 minutes early; dress business casual; bring ID.
5. Sit silently in the gallery; debrief after.

value.title: Show up to a rural-Florida 50501 county action
value.description:
1. Follow @collier50501.bsky.social.
2. Find the next action in the feed.
3. Show up; even 5 people at a courthouse in a red county matters.
4. Wear bright clothing for visibility.
5. Post the photo with the headcount.

value.title: Join 50501 New York's Times Square Saturday rally
value.description:
1. Follow @50501newyork.bsky.social.
2. RSVP the next Saturday rally from the pinned schedule.
3. Take the 1/2/3/7/N/Q/R to Times Square-42nd.
4. Bring a sign; meet at the red TKTS steps.
5. Post a wide-shot photo afterward.

value.title: Research what 'Hands Off the Sub' actually covers and share findings
value.description:
1. Visit @handsoffthesub.bsky.social and skim 30 most recent posts.
2. Identify the campaign's specific issue area (SNAP, transit, public housing).
3. Post your summary on Bluesky tagging the account for verification.
4. Share the verified summary in your local action chat.
5. Update if scope shifts.

value.title: Post a Threads thread breaking down your rep's last 5 votes
value.description:
1. Open govtrack.us; pull your rep's last 5 floor votes.
2. Open Threads; start a new thread.
3. Post one vote per reply: bill, what it does, how they voted.
4. End the thread with: 'Call them at [local number]. Script: [one line].'
5. Quote-thread it forward weekly when the next vote drops.

value.title: Post a copy-paste call script for today's senate vote
value.description:
1. Write a 4-line phone script naming the bill, the ask, a thank-you, and your zip-code.
2. Post the script on Threads with the local-office numbers for the 8 swing-vote senators.
3. Pin the post.
4. Update the script every Monday morning during session.
5. Reply to commenters who say 'called.'

value.title: Cross-post the UWD Here-to-Stay hotline on X with your city tag
value.description:
1. Post on X: 'ICE Hotline: 1-844-363-1423. Save this number.'
2. Tag @UnitedWeDream.
3. Tag your city or county.
4. Pin to your profile.
5. Refresh and repost monthly.

value.title: Submit a photo of your hand-made sign to @ahouseunited Bluesky for the next remix
value.description:
1. Make a sign for the next mass day.
2. Photograph it flat against a wall.
3. DM @ahouseunited.bsky.social with the file.
4. License it Creative Commons.
5. Watch other cities print it.

value.title: Reshare your state 50501 coordinator's daily post every morning
value.description:
1. Follow your state 50501 coordinator on Threads.
2. Each morning, reshare their first post of the day.
3. Add a one-line comment: 'NY action this Saturday — who's in?'
4. Tag two friends in your state.
5. Repeat 7 days a week.

value.title: Open an RSVP thread for your city's next anti-Trump march
value.description:
1. Two weeks before the march, post: 'RSVP below if you'll be at [city] [date] [time].'
2. Pin the thread.
3. Reply to each RSVP with the meet-spot and what to bring.
4. Reshare on the morning of the march with the closing comment 'see you in 2 hours.'
5. Post the headcount afterward.

value.title: Organize a 6-person postcard-write cohort over Threads DMs
value.description:
1. Post: 'Looking for 5 people for a postcard night next Sunday.'
2. Add the first 5 commenters to a Threads DM.
3. Pick the host, date, and the Indivisible target list.
4. Run the night.
5. Post the photo of the addressed stack.

value.title: Pin a Threads fundraiser post for your local bond fund all month
value.description:
1. Pick your local immigrant bond fund.
2. Write a Threads post with a $500/month goal and link.
3. Pin it for the month.
4. Update progress weekly with a comment.
5. Thank donors publicly when the goal hits.

value.title: Postcard your state legislator with a verified 50501 ask
value.location: Online to get ask; in person to mail
value.description:
1. Follow your state 50501 coordinator on Bluesky.
2. Pull this week's verified ask from a pinned post.
3. Write 5 postcards to your state legislators with the ask.
4. Mail today.
5. Post a photo of the addressed stack tagged at the coordinator.

value.title: Post the one-sentence bird-dog question you'll ask your senator this week
value.description:
1. Pick one bill or appointment your senator is voting on.
2. Write a 25-word question naming the bill and asking for a yes-or-no commitment.
3. Post it on Threads, tagged at the senator.
4. Commit publicly: 'I'm asking this at their next town hall.'
5. Cross-post to IG and X.

value.title: Post the week's federal-rule comment deadlines as a thread
value.description:
1. Pull the next 7 closing-soon dockets from regulations.gov.
2. Post each as a reply in one Threads thread with: agency, what the rule does, deadline.
3. Pin the thread for the week.
4. Reply with a sample 2-line comment per docket.
5. Refresh every Sunday night.

value.title: Drop your city's Saturday Tesla Takedown spot on Threads at noon Friday
value.description:
1. Confirm the Saturday meet time with your local chapter.
2. Post the address, time, and a sign suggestion on Threads at noon Friday.
3. Pin it.
4. Reply to anyone tagging a friend with the time again.
5. Repost the photo Saturday evening.

value.title: Launch a private Facebook group for resistance neighbors on your block
value.description:
1. Create a private FB group named '[Your block] Resistance.'
2. Set rules: anti-Trump-admin focus, no doxxing, no harassment, share local actions only.
3. Invite 10 neighbors you know are with you.
4. Pin the next 50501 / Hands Off / Tesla Takedown action.
5. Post a weekly digest of local actions.

value.title: Project a 4-letter message onto a building or overpass in your city
value.location: In person — your local highway overpass, federal building wall, or large blank facade after dark
value.description:
1. Pick a short message no longer than four words: 'NO KINGS', 'HANDS OFF', 'FEED THE KIDS', 'ICE OUT'.
2. Borrow or buy a portable projector capable of at least 3000 lumens (look for used theater rentals).
3. Scout a wall that faces a major roadway or pedestrian area, after dark, with low ambient light.
4. Drive or bike up, set up the projector inside a vehicle or on a tripod, project for 5-15 minutes, document with photo and video, then leave.
5. Post the photo with #LightBrigade and the city tag so other crews can repeat the action.

value.title: Sign up for Climate Defiance's free direct-action training
value.description:
1. Visit climatedefiance.org and click Action School at the top.
2. Pick the next Saturday session and submit your email.
3. Block 4 hours on a Saturday morning, show up on camera, and complete the three role-plays they assign.
4. After the training, opt in to the city you live in so they ping you the next time they bird-dog a senator near you.

value.title: Cancel your Amazon Prime account and post the reason on social media
value.description:
1. Go to amazon.com/your-account, click Membership, then End Membership.
2. Click through the three retention screens; confirm cancellation.
3. Take a screenshot of the confirmation page.
4. Post the screenshot with one sentence naming Bezos, the Washington Post non-endorsement, and the federal contracts on the line.
5. Tag two friends and ask them to do the same this week.

value.title: Trade in your Tesla for any other EV and post the receipt
value.location: In person — any non-Tesla EV dealership
value.description:
1. Get an online trade-in quote from at least two non-Tesla dealers (Hyundai, Kia, Polestar, Ford, Chevy).
2. Pick the best offer and bring your Tesla in for trade.
3. Drive home in a non-Tesla, and snap a photo of the keys and the trade-in paperwork.
4. Post the photo with one sentence about why — 'Musk runs DOGE; I am out.'
5. Tag the dealership and #TeslaTakedown so others see a real receipt.

value.title: Host an Indivisible postcard-write night at your kitchen table
value.description:
1. Order a stack of postcards and the current Indivisible state-target list from indivisible.org/postcards.
2. Invite 4-8 friends for a Sunday afternoon.
3. Provide snacks, pens, and one printed script per person.
4. Have everyone write postcards for 90 minutes; address, stamp, stack.
5. Drop the stack in a USPS box on the way home together.

value.title: Save the Here to Stay ICE hotline in your phone right now
value.description:
1. Open contacts on your phone.
2. Add a new contact named 'United We Dream ICE Hotline'.
3. Save the number 1-844-363-1423.
4. Star it so it surfaces fast in an emergency.
5. Text the number to two friends and ask them to do the same.

value.title: Build a hand-held LED letter sign for an overpass light brigade
value.location: In person — workshop at home, then deploy on a highway overpass at dusk
value.description:
1. Cut a 2ft x 2ft square of foamcore or thin plywood for one letter.
2. Hot-glue battery-powered string lights into the outline of a single bold letter, A-Z.
3. Repeat with friends, one person per letter, so you can spell a 4-8 letter word.
4. Coordinate a meet-time on an overpass at rush hour or 8-9pm; stand shoulder to shoulder holding the letters facing oncoming traffic.
5. Stay 60-90 minutes, leave nothing behind.

value.title: Disrupt a Trump-cabinet fundraiser or speech with a Climate Defiance-style banner read
value.location: In person — hotel ballroom, university auditorium, or industry gala where a cabinet member is speaking
value.description:
1. Buy a single ticket to the public event, or find the protest-area address on the venue's website.
2. Smuggle in a 4ft folded banner stating one demand and the official's name.
3. Wait until the official takes the podium, stand up, unfurl the banner, and read a single 30-second statement loud enough for the room to hear.
4. Let security walk you out; do not resist; have a buddy filming from a different row.
5. Post the clip with the official's name and the bill or policy they are advancing.

value.title: Leaflet outside an Amazon warehouse gate during a shift change
value.location: In person — the public sidewalk outside an Amazon fulfillment center near you
value.description:
1. Find the nearest Amazon fulfillment center on Google Maps; look for an MD- or BWI-style 4-letter code.
2. Print 200 quarter-sheet flyers naming the local union drive and one demand: paid heat breaks, mandatory bathroom access, ICE-collaboration ban.
3. Stand on the public sidewalk 30 minutes before shift change (6am or 6pm).
4. Hand a flyer to every worker walking in or out; do not block traffic; smile and say hi.
5. Leave when shift change ends, take your trash with you, and pin the location for the next crew.

value.title: Tuck an anti-Musk bumper sticker on a Tesla at a Supercharger
value.location: In person — a public Tesla Supercharger station
value.description:
1. Print or buy a stack of small magnetic 'I bought this before we knew' bumper plaques.
2. Stop by a Supercharger when 3-5 Teslas are charging.
3. Place one plaque under each windshield wiper — do not stick or damage anything.
4. Walk away. Do not engage drivers.
5. Photograph one row of cars showing the plaques and post anonymously.

value.title: Call your senator's local office about today's named bill
value.description:
1. Open the Indivisible app or weekly call sheet; pick the bill of the week.
2. Save your two senators' local office numbers — not DC — to your phone contacts.
3. Call at 10am or 4pm local time when a staffer will pick up.
4. Read the one-line script: name, city, ask for a yes or no commitment on the bill, thank the staffer.
5. Mark it done on the Indivisible app or in your own tracker.

value.title: Bird-dog your House rep at their next town hall with a video question
value.location: In person — your House representative's next town hall or public event
value.description:
1. Find your rep's next town hall, ribbon-cutting, or coffee meet on their official schedule, their campaign social, or town-hall-project.com.
2. Write a question one sentence long, naming a specific vote and asking them to commit to a yes or no on the next one.
3. Bring a friend to film at chest height in landscape orientation — do not hide the phone.
4. When you are called on, stand, state your name and city, ask the question, and refuse to be cut off until they answer.
5. Post the clip within two hours on TikTok and Instagram with the rep's name and district tagged.

value.title: Show up Saturday afternoon to your nearest Refuse Fascism march
value.location: In person — your nearest big-city plaza on a Saturday afternoon
value.description:
1. Search 'Refuse Fascism [your city]' on Instagram to find the local chapter and the exact 2pm Saturday spot.
2. Wear black, comfortable shoes, and bring a friend.
3. Show up at start time, take a sign they hand out, and march the route.
4. If you cannot march, hand out flyers from the sidewalk for one hour.

value.title: Picket a Tesla dealership this Saturday in your city
value.location: In person — your local Tesla showroom or service center
value.description:
1. Find a Tesla dealership or service center within driving distance.
2. Search '[your city] Tesla Takedown' on Instagram to find a local chapter and the exact start time.
3. If no local chapter exists, show up solo with a sign Saturday 11am-2pm and post a photo asking who is in.
4. Bring a sign that names Musk's role in DOGE or his Trump-administration ties.
5. Stand on the public sidewalk, smile, wave, hand out flyers if you have them.

value.title: Attend Seattle Indivisible's Tuesday night chapter meeting
value.location: In person or hybrid — Seattle (Capitol Hill rotating venues)
value.description:
1. DM @seattleindivisible on Instagram to confirm the week's venue and Zoom link.
2. Show up at 6:30pm Tuesday with a notebook and a calendar.
3. Sign the welcome sheet, sit with strangers, and commit to one task before you leave: postcard write night, doorknock shift, town hall buddy.
4. Add the next chapter meeting to your calendar before you walk out.

value.title: Print a Know Your Rights card and carry it in your wallet
value.description:
1. Visit unitedwedream.org/kyr and download the bilingual Know Your Rights card.
2. Print one for yourself, one for everyone in your household, and 10 extras.
3. Fold and slip one into your wallet behind your ID.
4. Hand the 10 extras to people at your workplace, place of worship, or laundromat.

value.title: Volunteer 3 hours as an immigration court watcher
value.location: In person — your nearest federal immigration court
value.description:
1. Search 'immigration court watch [your city]' for the local volunteer pipeline (NYC: NYIFUP, LA: ImmDef, Chicago: NIJC).
2. Complete the 1-hour orientation by Zoom.
3. Sign up for one 3-hour court morning per month.
4. On the day, dress business casual, bring a pen and ID, sit silently, and fill out the court-watch form for every case you see.
5. Submit the form by 5pm the same day.

value.title: Film an ICE stop from a safe distance and send the clip to local rapid response
value.description:
1. Stay on a public sidewalk at least 25 feet away from the activity.
2. Film in landscape; narrate location, time, and what you see in plain language.
3. Do not approach agents and do not interfere.
4. Send the clip to your city's rapid response number (search '[your city] ICE rapid response') within 30 minutes.
5. If a community member is detained, share the clip with their family only with their consent.

value.title: Host a Moral Monday livestream watch party
value.location: In person — your home or local church basement
value.description:
1. Pick the next Moral Monday date and reserve a room.
2. Invite 8-15 neighbors by text or Signal.
3. Stream the PPC livestream on a TV; provide coffee and a sign-in clipboard.
4. Pause 30 minutes for a structured discussion: which action do we take this week?
5. Send a follow-up text within 24 hours with one ask and one calendar invite.

value.title: Show up to the next 50501 mass day of action in your state capital
value.description:
1. Open the @50501movement linktree and find your state coordinator.
2. Save the date for the next national day (currently teased for June 27: 'All of U.S. 250').
3. Carpool to your state capital at the listed start time.
4. Bring a sign that names one of the seven 50501 demands.
5. Stay for the speakers; sign the local follow-up sheet before you leave.

value.title: Organize a school walkout on the next gun-violence anniversary
value.location: In person — your high school or college
value.description:
1. Pick a date — e.g., Parkland anniversary, Uvalde anniversary, or the next 50501 youth day.
2. Download the March For Our Lives walkout toolkit (marchforourlives.com/walkout).
3. Recruit 5 student organizers; coordinate the route from classroom to the front lawn.
4. At the walkout time, leave class quietly, gather on the lawn for 17 minutes of silence.
5. End with one student reading the names; record audio and post.

value.title: Hang a banner from a highway overpass during morning rush
value.location: In person — a pedestrian-safe highway overpass
value.description:
1. Choose an overpass with a pedestrian walkway, near merge traffic so cars slow down.
2. Paint a 6ft x 3ft banner with 4-5 letters max ('NO KINGS', 'TAX MUSK').
3. Use grommets and zip ties; arrive at 6:30am with two helpers.
4. Tie the banner securely to the railing facing oncoming traffic; stay for 60-90 minutes.
5. Untie and roll it up before highway patrol arrives; document with a wide shot from below.

value.title: Host a one-hour Indivisible house meeting with 6 neighbors
value.description:
1. Download the Indivisible house-meeting agenda (indivisible.org/resource/house-meeting).
2. Invite 6 neighbors by phone, not text.
3. Print 6 copies of the agenda.
4. Run the meeting in exactly 60 minutes: introductions, one ask, one commitment.
5. Send the meeting notes to attendees and the local chapter within 24 hours.

value.title: Join the next Moral Monday sit-in at your state capitol
value.location: In person — your state capitol building
value.description:
1. Visit poorpeoplescampaign.org and click your state chapter.
2. RSVP to the next Moral Monday; transport sometimes provided.
3. Show up at the capitol steps at 10am, wear black or PPC vest.
4. Follow trained marshal signals; sing, march, sit if you have planned for arrest.
5. Stay through the closing prayer and head home together.

value.title: Stage a Gays Against Guns die-in at your next pride or political event
value.location: In person — a public plaza, parade route, or steps of a legislator's office
value.description:
1. DM @gaysagainstguns to find or start a local chapter.
2. Recruit 20 friends. Get one white veil per person from a fabric store.
3. Pick a public spot at noon on a Saturday. Lie down silently for 9 minutes.
4. A buddy reads names of recent gun-violence victims aloud over a speaker.
5. Stand, walk away in single file, post one wide-shot photo without faces.

value.title: Join your local Sunrise hub and commit to one Wednesday call
value.location: In person or online — 200+ Sunrise hubs across the US
value.description:
1. Visit sunrisemovement.org/hubs and search your zip code.
2. Click 'Join this hub'; you'll get an email with the next meeting time.
3. Show up to a Wednesday call with your camera on.
4. Volunteer for the smallest task on the list: notes, art, food.
5. Add the next two meetings to your calendar before you log off.

value.title: Host a reproductive-rights candlelight vigil on your block
value.location: In person — your front porch, a public park, or church steps
value.description:
1. Pick a Sunday evening and a public-safe spot.
2. Invite the block by flyer, Nextdoor, and group text.
3. Buy 30 cheap candles in glass jars.
4. At sunset, light candles, hand out the next-action calling sheet, give a 3-minute statement.
5. Stay 45 minutes, collect contact info, and follow up within 48 hours.

value.title: Write a 200-word letter to your local paper about today's named bill
value.description:
1. Pick today's Indivisible bill of the week.
2. Open your local paper's letters-to-the-editor submission form.
3. Write 200 words: your city, the bill number, the local impact, and a named ask to your House rep.
4. Submit before 9am on Tuesday — best print-rate window for most local papers.
5. Post the submitted text to your social with the rep's handle.

value.title: Donate $50 to your local immigrant-bond fund
value.description:
1. Search '[your city] bond fund' — most major cities have one (NYC: Envision Freedom Fund; LA: Inland Coalition; SF: SF Public Defender Bond Fund).
2. Donate $50 or what you can.
3. Set up a monthly recurring $10.
4. Share the donation page with one friend who has the means.

value.title: Recruit a protest buddy and exchange jail-support info before you march
value.description:
1. Text one friend who is willing to be your buddy for the next protest.
2. Trade: full legal name, date of birth, emergency contact, and any meds you need.
3. Save the National Lawyers Guild jail support number for your city in both phones (NYC: 212-679-6018).
4. Agree on a meet point and a 'we're out' code phrase.
5. Both delete the conversation after the protest if you want.

value.title: Volunteer as a 50501 march marshal in your state
value.description:
1. Visit fiftyfifty.one (50501 movement site) and click Volunteer.
2. Pick the Marshal track and attend the 90-minute Zoom training.
3. Show up 90 minutes before the next mass day; pick up your vest and radio.
4. Walk your assigned block; help with crowd flow and water; de-escalate calmly.
5. Stay 60 minutes after dispersal for the marshal debrief.

value.title: Help five friends register to vote at a Black Voters Matter house party
value.description:
1. Download the Black Voters Matter house-party kit (blackvotersmatterfund.org).
2. Pick a weeknight and invite 6-10 friends for snacks.
3. Set up a laptop with vote.org open.
4. Walk each person through registration or registration update on the spot.
5. Take a photo with the BVM logo and post; tag the hosts of two house parties to come.

value.title: Chalk-bomb your downtown sidewalks with one demand
value.location: In person — sidewalks outside city hall, federal courthouse, or major plaza
value.description:
1. Buy a 3-pound box of fat sidewalk chalk in 6 colors.
2. Pick one short message: 'TAX MUSK', 'NO KINGS', 'STOP ICE'.
3. Go out before sunrise, work the sidewalk in 4-foot block letters every 30 feet.
4. Photograph each panel as you go; do not chalk on private property.
5. Post a contact sheet of 9 panels and tag the city.

value.title: Cook and deliver a meal to a family whose member was detained by ICE
value.location: In person — a household in your city
value.description:
1. Sign up with your local immigrant-defense network (NYIFUP, ImmDef, NIJC, or a local church coalition).
2. Pick a family from the active-cases list.
3. Cook one large dish that travels well: rice and beans, lasagna, soup.
4. Drop it off with a note and a contact number for follow-up help.
5. Do not photograph the family or the home.

value.title: Sign up as a poll worker for the next election in your county
value.location: In person — your county polling place
value.description:
1. Visit powerthepolls.org and enter your zip.
2. Submit the application for the next election.
3. Complete the county-mandated 2-hour training.
4. Show up at 5:30am on election day; stay through close + count.
5. Photograph your training certificate and post; tag a friend who could do it next time.

value.title: Read a 2-minute public comment at your school board against book bans
value.location: In person — your local school district board room
value.description:
1. Find the next board meeting on the district website.
2. Sign up for public comment by the deadline (usually 24-48 hours before).
3. Write a 2-minute statement naming a specific banned book and the local kids affected.
4. Show up, speak slowly, look at the board members, sit down.
5. Record your comment and post; tag two parents who could do it next month.

value.title: Host an anti-Musk movie or doc night with 8 friends
value.description:
1. Pick one short film or doc segment on Musk, DOGE, or labor at Tesla (45 min max).
2. Invite 8 friends for a Sunday evening.
3. Watch together; pause once for a 5-minute reflection.
4. After: hand out 8 'I cancelled Prime / sold my Tesla' commitment cards.
5. Collect the cards; text the group in 7 days for accountability.

value.title: Print and post 50501's seven-demand toolkit at your local library
value.description:
1. Download the 50501 seven-demand one-pager from fiftyfifty.one.
2. Print 20 copies in color if possible.
3. Walk your neighborhood; pin one to every community bulletin board (library, cafe, laundromat, co-op).
4. Photograph each pin location for the local chapter.
5. Refill at the same boards every 2 weeks.

value.title: Mail a thank-you postcard to your state AG for the latest Trump-admin lawsuit
value.location: Online to find address, then a postcard
value.description:
1. Find your state AG's mailing address (most have a P.O. box on their .gov site).
2. Buy three blank postcards.
3. Write one short thank-you naming the specific lawsuit (DOGE data, Title IX, deportation).
4. Sign, stamp, and drop in a USPS box today.
5. Photograph one card and post; tag your AG.

value.title: Print 200 anti-MAGA stickers and pass them out at the next show
value.location: In person — a concert, sports game, parade, or college campus
value.description:
1. Design a 2x2 sticker with one short message ('NO KINGS', 'TAX MUSK', 'ICE OUT').
2. Order 200 from Sticker Mule or a local print shop (under $30).
3. Carry them in your jacket pocket to your next event.
4. Hand one to anyone who makes eye contact; do not pitch.
5. Reorder when you run out.

value.title: Run a $500 birthday Facebook fundraiser for your local bond fund
value.description:
1. On Facebook or Instagram, create a birthday/anniversary fundraiser.
2. Set the goal at $500 and the recipient to your local bond fund.
3. Write one paragraph: who the fund bonds out and why right now.
4. Pin the post and remind once a week until it hits the goal.
5. Thank donors publicly when you close the fundraiser.

value.title: Print and stake a Protect Trans Kids yard sign on your lawn
value.location: In person — your front yard, porch, or window
value.description:
1. Order a yard sign from a local print shop — 18x24, double-sided, with H-stake.
2. If you can't get a sign, print the message on 11x17 and laminate.
3. Stake or tape it where it faces street traffic.
4. Snap a photo, post, and ask one neighbor to do the same.

value.title: Run a resistance table at your farmers market for one Saturday
value.location: In person — your local farmers market or community fair
value.description:
1. Email the market manager and reserve a free non-profit table for one Saturday.
2. Bring a folding table, two chairs, a tablecloth, and the Indivisible / 50501 / United We Dream flyers stacked.
3. Set out a clipboard for emails and a poster with one ask.
4. Wave to everyone, hand out flyers, do not lecture.
5. Email the captured emails to the local chapter within 24 hours.

value.title: Throw a sign-making party the night before the next mass protest
value.location: In person — your living room or backyard
value.description:
1. Invite 6 friends the night before the next 50501 / Hands Off / Tesla Takedown action.
2. Buy a stack of posterboard, paint pens, and tempera paints.
3. Each person makes one big sign with a single short message.
4. Take a group photo with the finished signs.
5. Caravan together to the march in the morning.

value.title: Shadow your MAGA House rep at every public event during recess week
value.location: In person — anywhere your rep appears that week
value.description:
1. Pull your rep's recess-week schedule from their official site and social.
2. Show up to every public event — ribbon cutting, parade, town hall, coffee meet.
3. Bring a sign and one question; film with a friend.
4. Be polite, persistent, and visible.
5. Compile a clip reel at the end of the week and post; tag the rep and local press.

value.title: Volunteer 2 hours on a United We Dream text bank shift
value.description:
1. Sign up at unitedwedream.org/volunteer for the next text bank.
2. Attend the 15-minute orientation Zoom.
3. Log into Spoke or Hustle on a laptop.
4. Send 200 texts using the script over 90 minutes.
5. Refer responses to the on-call rapid-response coordinator.

value.title: Buy a stack of banned books and donate them to a Little Free Library
value.location: In person — your local Little Free Library or community center
value.description:
1. Pull the current ALA top-ten-banned list.
2. Buy 3-5 paperbacks from bookshop.org (not Amazon).
3. Stamp or sticker each with 'Banned. Read it anyway.'
4. Stock the books in three Little Free Libraries in your zip code.
5. Photograph the books in place and post; tag the local school board.

value.title: Cover an overnight jail-support shift for arrested protesters
value.location: In person — sidewalk outside your county or city jail
value.description:
1. Contact your local National Lawyers Guild chapter to be added to the jail-support list.
2. When the call goes out, sign up for an overnight slot (10pm-6am).
3. Bring water, snacks, a phone charger, and a notebook.
4. Stay on the public sidewalk; greet released protesters by name; get them a ride.
5. Log names, charges, and release times on the NLG sheet.

value.title: March in your Pride parade as a Gays Against Guns silent block
value.location: In person — your local Pride parade route
value.description:
1. Register Gays Against Guns with your Pride org (or DM @gaysagainstguns to join an existing block).
2. Recruit 20 marchers; provide white veils.
3. Walk the parade silently in a tight block.
4. Hand out cards naming MAGA-backed gun policy and one ask.
5. Repeat for every Pride weekend in June.

value.title: Skip Prime Day and buy the same item from an independent shop
value.description:
1. Make your shopping list for Prime Day in advance.
2. For each item, find an independent or worker-owned alternative — bookshop.org, local hardware store, etc.
3. Buy it from the alt seller and screenshot the receipt total.
4. Post the receipt with one sentence: 'Spent $X anywhere but Amazon today.'
5. Tag two friends who will commit to do the same on day two.

value.title: Deliver water and snacks to volunteers at the next protest
value.location: In person — the protest staging area
value.description:
1. Find the staging spot for the next local action via the chapter's stories.
2. Buy: two cases of bottled water, a box of granola bars, a bag of fruit.
3. Show up 30 minutes before kickoff; find the marshal in charge of logistics.
4. Drop off the supplies; offer to do another run mid-day.
5. Stay or go; either is help.

value.title: Canvas one neighborhood for Poor People's Campaign on a Saturday morning
value.location: In person — a target neighborhood in your city
value.description:
1. Sign up for your state PPC chapter's next canvass.
2. Show up at the staging location at 9am Saturday with comfortable shoes.
3. Pair with a buddy; complete the 30-minute training.
4. Knock 50 doors over 3 hours; use the issue script.
5. Return to staging at 12:30pm with your turf and notes.

value.title: Wheatpaste 'Tax Musk' posters in your downtown overnight
value.location: In person — utility poles and construction barriers downtown
value.description:
1. Print 50 11x17 posters with one image and one demand.
2. Mix wheatpaste: 1 cup flour + 4 cups water, simmer to a gravy.
3. Go out between 1am-4am with a partner.
4. Brush wheatpaste on a utility pole, slap the poster, brush over the top.
5. Repeat for 50 surfaces; do not paste on private storefronts.

value.title: Photograph unmarked vehicles parked at ICE staging sites
value.location: In person — publicly visible federal-lot or hotel parking lots
value.description:
1. Find the public ICE field-office address; identify any overflow hotel parking they use.
2. Drive or bike past in daylight on a public street.
3. Photograph license plates and vehicle types from the public sidewalk.
4. Upload the photos to the regional ICE-watch shared folder (NYC: ICE Out NYC; LA: AFSC; SF: SF Rapid Response).
5. Do not approach, photograph individual agents' faces, or engage.

value.title: Move your checking account from a fossil-funding bank to a local credit union
value.description:
1. Find a local credit union or community bank (search 'switch from Chase/Wells/Citi/BofA' on bankforgood.org).
2. Open the new account online or in person.
3. Move direct deposit and auto-pays.
4. Close your old account in person and ask for a paper closing statement.
5. Post a photo of the closing statement with one sentence on why.

value.title: Spend an hour cleaning court-watch data for your local immigration nonprofit
value.description:
1. DM your local court-watch project asking if they need data entry.
2. Get the cleaning checklist (de-duplicate cases, normalize judge names, flag missing fields).
3. Spend one focused hour in Google Sheets or Airtable.
4. Commit to the same slot weekly for 4 weeks.
5. If you find a pattern — e.g., a specific judge denying at 95% — flag it to the project lead.

value.title: Drop off a thank-you cake to a unionized Amazon warehouse
value.location: In person — the union outpost near a unionized Amazon facility (JFK8 in Staten Island; LDJ5 in NJ; ALB1 in Albany)
value.description:
1. Order or bake a sheet cake big enough for 30 workers.
2. Coordinate with the local union outpost on day and time.
3. Drop the cake off on the sidewalk outside the union trailer.
4. Spend 30 minutes asking what the workers need next.
5. Post a wide shot of the cake without faces.

value.title: Take a free 1-hour bystander de-escalation training online
value.description:
1. Visit righttobe.org and click Bystander Intervention.
2. Sign up for the next free 1-hour Zoom training.
3. Attend with camera on; complete the role-plays.
4. Save the 5Ds card to your phone home screen.
5. Post the certificate of completion and tag a friend to take the next session.

value.title: Subscribe to Indivisible's weekly action email and do one action a week
value.description:
1. Visit indivisible.org/take-action.
2. Enter your email and your zip.
3. Add Indivisible to your inbox safe-senders list so it isn't promotioned out of view.
4. Each Monday open the email and do at least one of the three actions.
5. Forward the email to one friend.

value.title: Give an elderly neighbor a ride to early voting
value.description:
1. Two weeks before early voting opens, knock on three doors and offer rides.
2. Print a card with the dates, hours, and your phone number.
3. Pick up your neighbor on the day they pick.
4. Drive them to the polling place, wait, drive them home.
5. Repeat for two more neighbors that week.

value.title: Sign and share the Choose Democracy stop-a-coup pledge
value.description:
1. Visit choosedemocracy.us and read the ten principles.
2. Sign the pledge with your real name and zip.
3. Download the wallet card (the ten principles fit on a 3x5).
4. Share the pledge link on your social and DM it to three friends.
5. Watch the 30-minute video that ships with the signup confirmation.

value.title: Follow your state's 50501 coordinator and turn on post notifications
value.description:
1. Open the @50501movement linktree on Instagram.
2. Find your state coordinator's handle.
3. Follow them and tap the bell icon for post notifications.
4. DM them once: introduce yourself, give your city, ask the next ask.

value.title: Bird-dog a Trump cabinet official at the airport if you spot them
value.location: In person — the airport when you happen to be flying
value.description:
1. Keep a 1-sentence question saved on your phone notes for every senior Trump-admin official.
2. If you spot one in person, walk up calmly, film with your other hand at chest height.
3. Ask the question once; if they ignore you, ask once more politely.
4. Stop filming when they walk away; do not chase.
5. Post the clip within 30 minutes with the official's name and the bill they are advancing.

value.title: Post a public pledge to show up to your jury duty in a Black 7-style trial
value.description:
1. Write a public Threads post: 'When jury duty arrives, I will show up.'
2. Pin it.
3. If you're called for a politically-charged trial, accept the seat.
4. Use the post as a small public commitment device.
5. Encourage friends to do the same.

value.title: Screenshot a MAGA rep's deleted post and add the receipts
value.description:
1. Set a Google alert on your House rep's name and X/Threads handle.
2. Use politwoops.eu (or archive.today) to find their deleted posts.
3. Screenshot one and post on Threads with the date and the receipts.
4. Tag the local paper.
5. Repeat with each new deletion.

value.title: Poll your state legislators on Threads: where do they stand on [bill]?
value.description:
1. Pick a state-level bill mirroring the Trump-administration agenda.
2. Find every state legislator's Threads handle.
3. Post one Threads asking each: 'Will you vote yes or no on [bill]?'
4. Tag them by name.
5. Track responses in a public Google Sheet and update weekly.

value.title: Quote a real bench statement from court watch in a Threads post
value.description:
1. Pull a non-identifying quote from your last court-watch shift.
2. Post on Threads with the judge's name and the date.
3. Anonymize the respondent.
4. Add one line on why this matters.
5. Tag the local court-watch lead for verification.

value.title: Post the Moral Monday livestream link 30 minutes before it starts
value.description:
1. Find this Monday's Moral Monday livestream link on the PPC site.
2. 30 minutes before it starts, post the link on Threads.
3. Add a one-line ask: 'Join from your couch — sit-in starts at noon.'
4. Reshare during the action.
5. Post the recording afterward.

value.title: RSVP and reshare the next Hands Off national mass call
value.description:
1. Find the next Hands Off national mass call on Threads (announced weekly).
2. RSVP through the link.
3. Reshare the post on Threads with one line: 'I'll be on Wednesday at 8.'
4. Show up to the call.
5. Repost the call's three action asks afterward.

value.title: Post a 'who wants to join a Sunrise hub' callout in your state
value.description:
1. Check sunrisemovement.org/hubs for your zip.
2. If no hub: post on Threads, 'Looking for 4 people in [your city] to start a Sunrise hub.'
3. Collect names in DMs.
4. Schedule the first meeting; share the date publicly.
5. Use the Sunrise hub-start toolkit.

value.title: Run a daily 'period-supply drop' progress thread
value.description:
1. Post a Threads daily-update on your period-supply drive.
2. Include the school's name with consent or a code (school A, B, C).
3. Post the count of boxes each day.
4. Tag local press for the close-out.
5. Repeat next month.

value.title: Name a Tesla institutional investor on Threads and call for a vote
value.description:
1. Pull a Tesla institutional holder (Vanguard, BlackRock, State Street) and find their next proxy vote.
2. Post on Threads: 'X holds 4% of Tesla. Vote against the Musk pay package on [date].'
3. Tag the investor's IR handle.
4. Repeat with three more holders.
5. Aggregate the responses (or non-responses) in a follow-up thread.

value.title: Quote one chilling sentence from a Trump-admin executive order on Threads daily
value.description:
1. Open the latest White House EO or DOGE memo.
2. Pull one short sentence (under 15 words) verbatim.
3. Post it on Threads with the source link.
4. Add one line of plain-language explanation.
5. Repeat daily for a week.

value.title: Run a public Threads poll on which mass day people will attend
value.description:
1. Use the Threads poll feature.
2. List the next three mass-day dates (50501, Hands Off, Tesla Takedown).
3. Pin for 7 days.
4. Post the result with one line: 'X people voted, see you Saturday.'
5. Repeat for the next mass day.

value.title: Quote one paragraph from the Hands Off organizing toolkit on Threads
value.description:
1. Download the Hands Off organizing toolkit.
2. Pick one paragraph that names a tactic anyone can do this week.
3. Quote it on Threads with the toolkit link.
4. Tag two friends.
5. Repeat with a different paragraph next week.

value.title: Film a 30-second explainer on one bill your rep is voting on this week
value.description:
1. Pick today's Indivisible bill of the week.
2. Open TikTok, hit the plus, film yourself for 30 seconds: bill name, what it does to you, what your rep is doing.
3. Use #ResistTrump #NoKings #BirdDog and tag your rep's TikTok handle if they have one.
4. Post; share to your IG story.
5. Reply to comments for the first hour to boost the algorithm.

value.title: Stitch a MAGA influencer's brag and fact-check it
value.description:
1. Open TikTok, find a MAGA influencer celebrating a Trump-admin win.
2. Stitch the first 5 seconds of their clip.
3. Add 25 seconds of you naming the specific harm with a source link in caption.
4. Use the source on screen with a screenshot for legitimacy.
5. Post and reply to bad-faith comments with the same source link.

value.title: Film your Tesla Takedown picket vertical for TikTok
value.location: In person — your local Tesla dealership
value.description:
1. Show up to the Saturday Tesla Takedown picket in your city.
2. Film vertical 15-second clips: the crowd, your sign, a chant.
3. Stitch them in TikTok with one caption: 'Week N at the [your city] Tesla.'
4. Hashtag #TeslaTakedown #DefundMusk #ResistTrump.
5. Post by 6pm same day to ride the weekend algorithm.

value.title: Greenscreen your rep's voting record and tag them
value.description:
1. Pull your House rep's last 10 votes on govtrack.us.
2. Open TikTok, use greenscreen with a screenshot of the votes.
3. Spend 45 seconds reading the three worst ones and naming who they hurt locally.
4. Tag the rep, your local paper, and one MAGA county chair.
5. Repost monthly.

value.title: Film your parent or grandparent explaining what the Medicaid cut means to them
value.description:
1. Sit down with a family member who relies on Medicaid, SNAP, or Social Security.
2. Ask one question and let them talk for 30-45 seconds.
3. Film vertical; minimal edits.
4. Caption with the specific bill or rule being changed.
5. Tag your House rep and senator.

value.title: Use the trending sound to cancel your Prime on camera
value.description:
1. Pick today's top trending sound (the algorithm gives 2x reach for the top three).
2. Film yourself opening amazon.com, navigating to end membership, clicking confirm.
3. Use that trending sound under it.
4. Caption with one line on Bezos and a CTA to do it next.
5. Post; reply 'why' to bait-comments with one source link.

value.title: Film a tutorial on how to safely document ICE activity
value.description:
1. Film 60 seconds standing on a public sidewalk (not at an actual ICE site).
2. Walk through: stay 25 feet away, film landscape, narrate time and place, report to local rapid response.
3. List the local rapid-response number on screen.
4. Caption with #ICEWatch and your city tag.
5. Pin to profile.

value.title: Film a tour of your block's Protect Trans Kids yard signs
value.description:
1. Walk your block and any neighbor blocks.
2. Film vertical of each Protect Trans Kids / Hate Has No Home Here / Refugees Welcome yard sign.
3. Edit them together with a 'we are not alone' caption.
4. Tag your zip code; tag the local school board.
5. Encourage one neighbor a week to add a sign.

value.title: Post a 60-second tutorial on how to make a No Kings banner
value.description:
1. Set up your phone on a tripod over a flat surface.
2. Film 60 seconds: laying out a 6ft canvas, drawing the letters, painting them in.
3. Voiceover with the supply list and total cost.
4. Caption with the next 50501 mass day date.
5. Pin the video to your profile during the run-up.

value.title: Host a 30-minute TikTok live debrief the night after a mass day
value.description:
1. Schedule a live for 8pm the night after the mass day.
2. Recap headcounts, best moments, and unfinished work.
3. Invite 3 local coordinators on as guests.
4. Open the floor: what should we do next?
5. End with one concrete next ask.

value.title: Film a meal-prep cooking video for a detained-family meal drop
value.description:
1. Time-lapse film yourself cooking a big batch of soup or rice and beans.
2. Voiceover the recipe and where the food is going (no family names).
3. End with the local mutual-aid network's signup link.
4. Caption: 'Comment IN if you'll do one drop next month.'
5. Reply to every IN comment with a direct signup link.

value.title: Walk and film the next mass-day route in your city
value.description:
1. Two days before the march, walk the planned route at the start time.
2. Film vertical with voiceover: where to meet, where the speakers will be, where the bathrooms are, where police will likely block.
3. Post the same night with the start time pinned in caption.
4. Share the link with the local coordinator for repost.
5. Pin to profile until the march.

value.title: Screen-record yourself calling your rep and post the audio
value.description:
1. Open your phone screen recorder.
2. Call your senator or rep's local office on speakerphone.
3. Use a 30-second script; thank the staffer.
4. Edit out only your phone number and personal info.
5. Post the recording with a caption: 'It took 90 seconds. Here's the script.'

value.title: Post a 15-second video naming your local bond fund and dropping the link
value.description:
1. Pick your local immigrant bond fund.
2. Film 15 seconds: name the fund, name one person they recently bonded out, drop the link in bio.
3. Use the trending sound for reach.
4. Pin to profile during a high-detention week.
5. Reply to comments with the donate link.

value.title: Edit a 30-second bird-dog clip from a Climate Defiance action and remix it
value.description:
1. Pull the most recent Climate Defiance bird-dog clip.
2. Edit a 30-second cut with a one-line caption naming the cabinet official.
3. Use a trending sound to ride the algorithm.
4. Tag the official; tag the bill they were asked about.
5. Post and pin during the official's news cycle.

value.title: Host a period-supplies drive on TikTok for a Title IX-impacted school
value.description:
1. Pick a local public middle/high school cut off from federal Title IX supplies.
2. Film a 30-second TikTok asking viewers to drop a box of pads/tampons at your door this week.
3. Coordinate with the school nurse for a discreet drop.
4. Film the box-stack delivery (no faces of students).
5. Repeat monthly.

value.title: Record your school board comment on book bans and post it
value.description:
1. Sign up for public comment at the next board meeting.
2. Read your 2-minute statement.
3. Have a friend film your comment from the floor.
4. Edit to 60 seconds; caption with the board members' names.
5. Tag the local paper and the district account.

value.title: Help maintain the public 50501 state coordinator roster on Bluesky
value.description:
1. Open the 50501 movement's pinned coordinator roster.
2. Verify your state's coordinator is still active (posted in last 14 days).
3. DM 50501movement.bsky.social with corrections.
4. Add any new local coordinators you discover.
5. Re-check monthly.

value.title: Sync ICE Watch alerts with your local rapid-response Signal group
value.description:
1. DM your local immigrant rapid-response network with the ICE Watch app screenshot.
2. Ask to be added to the alerts Signal group.
3. Set up Signal notifications to bypass do-not-disturb.
4. When an alert fires, forward to two more trusted neighbors.
5. Do not screenshot Signal groups publicly.

value.title: Use the news of the week to explain one tax policy on Musk
value.description:
1. Pick one current proposal: wealth tax, billionaire minimum, capital gains step-up.
2. Film 60 seconds explaining: what it is, what it would cost Musk specifically, what your House rep voted.
3. Use a chart on screen if possible.
4. Hashtag #TaxMusk #DefundMusk.
5. Tag your rep.

value.title: Share your state 50501 coordinator's TikTok to your story every week
value.description:
1. Find your state coordinator via the @50501movement linktree.
2. Once a week, pick one of their videos and share it to your story.
3. Add a one-line CTA: 'next action [date]'.
4. DM the coordinator that you shared it.
5. Repeat weekly.

value.title: Repost your state 50501 coordinator's Bluesky post daily
value.description:
1. Follow your state coordinator on Bluesky (find via @50501movement.bsky.social pinned post).
2. Each morning, repost their first post.
3. Quote-repost one a week with the action ask.
4. Add your state coordinator to a 'state actions' list.
5. Open the list daily.

value.title: Quote-tweet your MAGA rep with the receipts on their last vote
value.description:
1. Find your rep's most recent tweet.
2. Quote-tweet with one line naming their last vote that contradicts the tweet.
3. Add a screenshot of govtrack.us as evidence.
4. Tag the local paper.
5. Replies; engage in the first hour for algorithm reach.

value.title: Amplify Climate Defiance's latest bird-dog clip on X within an hour
value.description:
1. Follow @climatedefiance with notifications on.
2. When a bird-dog clip drops, retweet within the first hour.
3. Quote-tweet with the cabinet official's name and the bill in question.
4. Tag the official's X handle.
5. Reply to bad-faith comments with one source link, then stop engaging.

value.title: Post your 50501 mass-day RSVP on X with your state tagged
value.description:
1. Find the next 50501 mass-day date.
2. Post on X: '[Your state] — going to the capitol on [date]. Who is in?'
3. Tag @50501Movement and your state coordinator.
4. Reply to anyone who lifts a hand with the meet spot.
5. Repost the day-of headcount.

value.title: Post a Tesla Takedown check-in on X tagged at the local chapter
value.description:
1. Show up to your local Tesla Takedown Saturday picket.
2. Take a wide-shot photo.
3. Post on X with location tagged.
4. Tag @TeslaTakedown and your city's chapter.
5. Reply with the next Saturday date.

value.title: Print a free protest-art template from A House United Bluesky
value.description:
1. Follow @ahouseunited.bsky.social.
2. Pick one of their pinned downloadable templates.
3. Print on 11x17 paper.
4. Mount on foamcore with spray adhesive.
5. Carry to the next march; photograph and post.

value.title: Tweet your Protect Trans Kids yard-sign photo at your state legislator
value.description:
1. Photograph your yard sign.
2. Post on X with one sentence.
3. Tag your state legislator and the @WomensMarch account.
4. Tag the local paper.
5. Reply with the next state-level bill on Title IX or trans care.

value.title: Join Tesla Takedown MN at the Eden Prairie or Maplewood location
value.location: In person — Eden Prairie or Maplewood Tesla locations
value.description:
1. Follow @teslatakedownmn.bsky.social.
2. Pick a Saturday from their pinned schedule.
3. Drive to Eden Prairie or Maplewood.
4. Bring a sign; check in with marshals.
5. Post a check-in photo Sunday morning.

value.title: Screenshot a MAGA rep's deleted X post and quote-tweet the receipts
value.description:
1. Set up a politwoops.eu alert for your House rep.
2. When a deletion fires, screenshot the deleted tweet.
3. Quote-tweet with the deletion timestamp and the original text.
4. Tag the local paper.
5. Pin one a month.

value.title: Show up to a No Kings Albany capitol-steps action
value.location: In person — NY State Capitol, Albany
value.description:
1. Follow @nokings-albany.bsky.social.
2. Pick the next capitol-day announced in their feed.
3. Carpool from Albany, Schenectady, or Troy.
4. Bring a sign.
5. Stay through speakers and write a follow-up post tagged at the NY governor.

value.title: Show up to Defund Musk's Tuesday Seattle Tesla showroom
value.description:
1. Follow @defundmusk.bsky.social.
2. Tuesday at 5:30pm: arrive at the showroom they've pinned.
3. Bring a sign.
4. Picket until 7pm.
5. Post the wide-shot Tuesday night.

value.title: Participate in the Hands Off deregulation comment day
value.description:
1. Follow Hands Off deregulation accounts on Bluesky for monthly comment day.
2. On the day, open regulations.gov to the target docket.
3. Write 200-300 original words on local impact.
4. Submit; screenshot.
5. Post the screenshot tagged at the rule's agency.

value.title: Join the PDX Tesla Takedown Saturday picket
value.description:
1. Follow @teslatakedownpdx.bsky.social for time and place each week.
2. Show up Saturday with a sign.
3. Stand on the public sidewalk.
4. Wave at honking cars; do not engage Tesla buyers.
5. Post a check-in photo to Bluesky tagging the chapter.

value.title: Post a check-in on Bluesky from the Tesla Takedown picket
value.description:
1. Show up Saturday to the Tesla Takedown in your city.
2. Post a check-in on Bluesky: city, headcount, one quote.
3. Tag the local chapter handle (e.g., @teslatakedownla.bsky.social).
4. Add #TeslaTakedown.
5. Reshare two other cities' check-ins from your timeline.

value.title: Post a solidarity photo to the Tesla Takedown UK Bluesky feed
value.description:
1. Take a photo with a Tesla Takedown sign from any city.
2. Tag @teslatakedownuk.bsky.social.
3. Add a one-line solidarity message.
4. Pin to your profile for the week.
5. Reshare the UK chapters' weekly photo back.

value.title: Duet a United We Dream Know Your Rights clip in your language
value.description:
1. Find a United We Dream KYR clip in English.
2. Open the duet option in TikTok.
3. Record yourself translating each line into your home language (Spanish, Mandarin, Haitian Creole, Arabic, Tagalog, etc.).
4. Caption with the hotline and a country-of-origin emoji.
5. Post; pin to your profile.

value.title: Go live on TikTok the night before a 50501 mass day
value.description:
1. The night before the mass day, schedule a TikTok live 8-9pm local.
2. Make a sign on camera; explain the event time, the route, transit details.
3. Take viewer questions for 20 minutes.
4. End with a clear ask: who is bringing a friend, who is bringing snacks, who is bringing water.
5. Save the live; clip the best 60 seconds and repost.

value.title: Film a 30-second climate strike walkout from your school or job
value.description:
1. Coordinate with 5 coworkers or classmates for the next national climate strike.
2. Walk out at the announced time; gather on the public sidewalk.
3. Film the walkout vertical from across the street.
4. Post within 1 hour with #ClimateStrike and your employer/school tagged.
5. Repeat at the next quarterly strike.

value.title: Post a 60-second safety tutorial on legal banner drops
value.description:
1. Film 60 seconds in your living room with banner laid out.
2. Cover: tie off both ends, weight the bottom, never lean over the rail, use a buddy.
3. Show two zip-tie attachment methods on a chair.
4. Caption: 'Drop a banner, not yourself.'
5. Pin to profile.

value.title: Follow the DemocracyTok creator pack and rewatch one explainer a day
value.description:
1. On TikTok, search 'DemocracyTok creator list 2026' and follow the 20 listed accounts.
2. Each morning, scroll your For You and rewatch one of their videos to 100%.
3. Like and save it.
4. Comment with one extra fact or source.
5. Repeat 5 days a week.
